name: Bathroom Safety
description: The bathroom is the most common room for senior falls. Explore grab bars, shower chairs, transfer benches, raised toilet seats, and non-slip mats — plus the guides that tie them together.

name: Bedroom Safety
description: Safe transfers in and out of bed, nighttime lighting, and reducing nighttime fall risk for aging adults.

name: Fall Prevention
description: A whole-home approach to reducing falls — the leading cause of injury for older adults — through products, home modifications, and routines.

name: Mobility & Transfer Aids
description: Aids that make moving, reaching, and transferring safer and less tiring for seniors — from walkers and canes to transfer benches and reachers.

name: Medical Alert Systems
description: Devices and services that summon help fast when a senior living independently needs it.

name: Medication Organization
description: Tools and routines that make managing daily medications safer and clearer.

name: Communication Devices
description: Phones and devices that keep seniors connected and able to call for help.

name: Daily Living & Comfort
description: Everyday aids that keep seniors comfortable and independent — from compression socks that ease tired legs to tools that make daily tasks easier.

name: Dementia & Wandering Safety
description: Tools that help families care for a loved one with dementia or Alzheimer’s at home — door and motion alarms, exit stoppers, and caregiver pagers that give precious seconds to respond.

name: Safety Gifts for Seniors
description: Thoughtful gifts for aging parents that quietly improve safety, comfort, and independence.
